name: Cookie Jar Gingersnaps
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. In a medium bowl, sift together flour, ground ginger, baking soda, cinnamon, cloves, and salt.
3. In a large bowl with an electric mixer, beat shortening until soft (about 1 minute).
4. Gradually add sugar and continue beating until the mixture is light and fluffy.
5. Beat in egg and molasses until well-blended.
6. Stir in the flour mixture until completely mixed.
7. Place a little sugar in a small bowl.
8. Scoop heaping teaspoonfuls of dough into the sugar, using your palms to roll them into 3/4-inch balls.
9. Drop the balls into the sugar and roll each one to cover its surface completely.
10. Place the cookies about 2 inches apart on ungreased baking sheets.
11. Bake until the cookies are slightly rounded and have a lightly browned top with small cracks.
12. Remove the baking sheets from the wire racks to cool slightly.
13. Using a metal pancake turner or palette knife, transfer the cookies to wire racks to cool completely.
14. Repeat with any remaining dough and sugar.
15. Store in airtight containers.
Source: "The Complete Cookie Book" by Elizabeth Wolf Cohen
Notes: Each cookie contains 70.4 calories (42.7% from fat) and has 3.4g of fat. The cookies were delicious and easy to make.
